rotation_cadence.fresh_refresh_target: refresh each hidden fresh family at least once per reporting quarter or before the next official evaluation window opens
rotation_cadence.test_window_boundary_rule: do not change the active hidden test packs inside a frozen official window unless contamination or critical evaluator invalidation forces retirement
rotation_cadence.canary_review_target: review canary freshness and exploit sensitivity before each new official window
rotation_cadence.minimum_rotation_review_frequency: review all active hidden families monthly even if no rotation is executed

retirement_triggers: confirmed hidden-content leakage or material inferability
official window closure followed by a successor pack set
critical evaluator bug that changes pack meaning materially
fresh successor promotion that makes the active pack stale or redundant
track imbalance or coverage drift severe enough to make the pack misleading

fresh_to_test_requirements: candidate scenarios must already exist in a hidden fresh pack with distinct ids and files
validation, lint, and smoke checks must pass on the candidate family
exploit review or canary review must not show unresolved benchmark-specific gaming risk
public test and fresh manifests must remain redacted and version-correct after promotion
test_successor_requirements: retire the superseded pack in the public changelog before accepting new leaderboard entries against the successor
publish the successor pack version in frozen active packs and the official window docs
keep legacy history visible rather than deleting prior pack references
metadata_update_requirements: update frozen active packs when the official window uses the new set
update benchmark status and task tracking when the rotation changes benchmark claims

notes: This policy makes rotation cadence explicit without forcing synthetic churn inside a frozen reporting window.
Fresh packs are short-window contamination pressure tools, not permanent side tables.
